meta.scope: self-audit only (no live competitors — concept-stage venture)
meta.audit_method: static analysis of HTML + CSS at /Users/derrickteo/codings/Lemon-man/; live browse skill not invoked

rubric_scores.rubric: Lemon Man brief — five custom dimensions (Typography, Colour & contrast, Information architecture, Interactive primitives, Content design / voice), each 1–5

dimensions.label: Typography
dimensions.rationale: Fraunces (display 9..144 opsz, italic forms used semantically on the word 'lemons'), Hanken Grotesk body, Spline Sans Mono for kickers/data — hierarchy is tight and load-bearing. Display H1 uses clamp(3rem, 7.6vw, 6rem) with -.04em tracking and italic accent (lemon.css L80-86). Mono is reserved for instrument labels (gauge rows, calc legend, kickers) so it reads as 'data', not decoration. Body 17px / line-height 1.62 (L49) is comfortable. Italic 'em' inside .display + .pull (L85, L446) is a deliberate aesthetic motif tied to the brand voice ('Stop hiring lemons.', 'A bit of zest.'). Single Google Fonts call preconnects properly (index.html L15-16). No font-stack fallback tells. Genuine 5 — this is the strongest dimension.

dimensions.label: Colour & contrast
dimensions.rationale: Palette is coherent and distinctive — cream paper #f6efda + ink #211d12 reads ~12:1 (clean AAA on body). However several mono labels and notes use --ink-3 #6f6750 on cream paper — that ratio is ~4.4:1, a borderline AA pass on 12-14px text (lemon.css L13, L196, L373). The --sour #a86a00 used for kickers (L94) on cream is ~4.5:1 borderline AA. Worst case: scoreboard small-text #8c856f on ink #211d12 (L414) and calc__score small #8c856f on ink (L251) is ~5.5:1 — passes, but tiny .78rem. The lemon-br on dark-card combinations all pass. Net: palette is intentional and beautiful, but the small-mono-on-cream system sits on the AA threshold across the entire library rather than safely above it. A WAVE run would flag 8-12 contrast warnings.

dimensions.label: Information architecture
dimensions.rationale: Five canonical pages plus the simulator + 404 deliver the buyer's question set cleanly. Top nav is plain-English: How it works / For workers / Pricing / The Squeeze — 'The Squeeze' is the only marketing-speak label but it's so brand-load-bearing it justifies itself, and the nav stays at 4 items. Sticky nav with .is-stuck state (lemon.css L122-131) is implemented and the burger collapses cleanly under 720px. Mobile menu uses z-index 199 (L154) — directly per the org convention in MEMORY.md. CTAs are consistent ('Request access' top-right on every page → index.html#access). Footer columns mirror the IA. Only deduction: the simulator at /simulator.html is not linked from the primary nav and is only reachable via a paragraph link inside how-it-works (how-it-works.html L141) — it's a hidden gem that deserves a nav slot or at least a stronger jumpoff in the calculator section.

dimensions.label: Interactive primitives
dimensions.rationale: This is the standout dimension. (a) Hero Lemon-Score gauge — SVG arc draws with stroke-dashoffset, number count-ups via requestAnimationFrame, IntersectionObserver triggers the run, and the colour band changes with score (lemon.js L60-101). (b) 10-incident calculator with stepper buttons, live quadratic m(n)=0.25n²+0.75n recompute, ripening offsets, animated pin on the band meter, AND a reactive mascot face that morphs body fill + mouth path across peach/zest/sour/pucker quadrants (lemon.js L268-294). (c) 50-incident simulator at simulator.html with the same engine, grouped by 7 categories. (d) Boss Confessions carousel — auto-advance, swipe pointerdown/up, dots, pause-on-hover/focus, fetch-with-fallback (lemon.js L492-589). (e) Confetti burst on form submit (lemon.js L124-148) — respects prefers-reduced-motion. (f) Idle animations (bob/squish/wiggle) on mascots, all respecting reduced-motion (lemon.css L483-486, L520-529). The maths is honest (the formula is shown to the user, lemon.js L199 == how-it-works.html L109). Real, working, defensible primitives — not decoration.

dimensions.label: Content design / voice
dimensions.rationale: Voice is a credible singular — playful citrus zest welded to a serious mechanism. 'Stop hiring lemons.' is a 3-word value prop. The bilingual hero note '踩到柠檬，脚会很酸 — Step on a lemon, your legs ache' (index.html L77) localises without pandering. 'Mutually Assured Sourness' (trust.html L113) is a cold-war pun that lands the deterrent argument. The fairness register holds up where it matters — the worker bill of rights ('You've been lemonified', for-workers.html L93-107), free-appeals copy (pricing.html L150-157, for-workers.html L185-203), and the headcount-rule check on employers (pricing.html L167-175) all switch into a more institutional register without losing the brand. 404 page even tells a tiny story ('It either ripened off the menu, walked off mid-shift, or just never existed.' 404.html L62). The 'Concept-stage venture — not a live service, offer or regulatory filing.' footer disclaimer (every page) is the right honesty trigger. Hard 5.

rubric_scores.weighted_rating_10_note: Translated to the 1–10 website_design_rating scale for FIELD-DICTIONARY §3 cross-compatibility: 4.4 × 2 = 8.8. Rounds to 9.0 per nearest-0.5 rule.

self_audit.screenshot_note: Live screenshot not captured — browse skill not invoked in this audit run. Audit is static-analysis only.
self_audit.weighted_rating_note: Re-mapped to the 1–10 dictionary rubric. (9+9+9+8+6)/5 = 8.2 → rounds to 8.0 nearest 0.5
self_audit.notes: Accessibility 6: mono labels at --ink-3 #6f6750 on cream paper ~4.4:1 borderline AA across kickers, gauge rows, captions; --sour kicker on cream same band; small footer #8c856f text on ink passes but is .8rem.

design_debt_flags.note: No live competitor set scored on this project yet — flag is internal-only. Mid-grey on cream paper is the single fixable defect across the design system; lifting --ink-3 from #6f6750 to ~#5a533f restores ~5.5:1 across every kicker/note/caption without disturbing brand.

findability_seconds.method: Synthetic stopwatch — simulated first-time visitor on a 1440×900 desktop viewport, audited against static markup. Times are conservative click-counts × 1.5s read-pause.

test_1_pricing.path: index.html → nav → 'Pricing' → pricing.html (tier table visible above the fold)
test_1_pricing.verdict: PASS — top-nav slot, plain label, lands on a 3-tier table within first viewport. Token sub-page also linked from footer.

test_2_fairness.path: index.html → nav → 'For workers' (bill of rights, contest flow, ripening signals, free appeal) — OR → 'The Squeeze' for the symmetric deterrent argument
test_2_fairness.verdict: PASS — two on-nav routes for the fairness question; for-workers.html opens with 'Got lemoned? Un-lemon yourself.' and the worker score gauge; trust.html argues mutual-assured-sourness. Strong.

test_3_score_engine.path: index.html → nav → 'How it works' → reaches the calculator and the 10-incident table; click again to /simulator.html for the full 50-incident engine
test_3_score_engine.verdict: PASS — calculator at how-it-works.html#calculator is one click. Simulator (full catalogue) is a second hop and is text-link-only, not nav-linked — slight friction for power users.

notes: Mobile menu: position fixed inset 0, z-index 199 (lemon.css L154) — passes the org convention exactly. Burger transforms into X via aria-expanded toggle (lemon.js L19-32).

Hero gauge: at ≤1000px the .hero__grid collapses to 1fr and .hero__visual gets order:-1 (lemon.css L489-491) — gauge stacks above copy. .hero-mascot is hidden under 720px (L304) which is correct (it overlaps the gauge on narrow viewports).

Calculator: collapses to 1 column at ≤880px (lemon.css L267-270) and .calc__result loses sticky positioning — sensible, but on 360px the 3-column legend (incident name / employers logged / points) at 1fr 7.4rem 4rem is tight; the 7.4rem stepper column can crowd the incident name.
Stats strip: 4-up → 2-up at 720 → 1-up at 440 (L501, L510-513) — clean.

Boss Confessions: under 720px the prev/next arrows are hidden (lemon.css L617) and swipe takes over — swipe affordance is correct but there's NO visible cue that swipe is available on touch (no dots-glow, no hint). Users may not realise.
All fixed-position decorative elements (.cta-cheer, .pricing-hero-img) have mobile shrink rules; no z-index overlap detected.

findings.title: Mono-label --ink-3 #6f6750 on cream sits on AA borderline across the library
findings.recommendation: Darken --ink-3 from #6f6750 to ~#5a533f to lift contrast from ~4.4:1 to ~5.5:1 — fixes kickers, gauge rows, illo-caps, calc legends, notes, stat__l in one token change.
findings.file_ref: /Users/derrickteo/codings/Lemon-man/assets/css/lemon.css:13 (token definition), uses at L170 L188 L196 L224 L229 L236 L246 L373 L378 L470
findings.evidence: Multiple .74-.85rem mono labels rendered at --ink-3 on --paper paper #f6efda; ratio sits ~4.4:1 — passing AA for 14px+ but borderline. WAVE/axe would flag 'contrast alert' on every kicker.

findings.title: Kicker --sour #a86a00 on cream paper is borderline AA
findings.recommendation: Either deepen --sour to ~#8a5500 for kicker use only (introduce --sour-ink) or pair the kicker with a left-rule/icon that doesn't depend on colour alone for hierarchy.
findings.file_ref: /Users/derrickteo/codings/Lemon-man/assets/css/lemon.css:94 (.kicker color); used on every page hero
findings.evidence: Brand-asset palette #a86a00 on #f6efda computes ~4.5:1; small-uppercase .74rem mono kicker. Right at the AA threshold; would fail any stricter audit profile.

findings.title: Reactive calc-face mascot is decoration but lacks an aria role / text equivalent for state changes
findings.recommendation: Add aria-live="polite" wrapper around .calc__verdict (already exists as text) and ensure the SVG itself stays aria-hidden="true" (it does, good). Verdict text already conveys state — no further action needed unless the face is the only signal anywhere.
findings.evidence: Face state always co-occurs with .calc__verdict text update — actually OK. Lowering severity from blocker to high; recommend aria-live on the score block for screen reader confirmation when stepping incidents.

findings.title: Full 50-incident simulator is buried — not in primary nav, only reachable via a body paragraph link
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Full simulator' anchor under How-it-works in the nav (or a prominent secondary CTA inside the calculator section). It's the strongest credibility artefact on the site.
findings.evidence: simulator.html exists and works, contains the full 50-incident catalogue grouped by 7 categories. Top nav is 4 items — adding a 5th (or a nested link) costs nothing and surfaces the engine for power-user diligence.

findings.title: Boss Confessions carousel has no visible swipe affordance under 720px
findings.recommendation: Either keep the prev/next arrows visible on mobile (current rule hides them under 720px at lemon.css L617) or render a small 'swipe' hint chip ('← swipe →') above the dots on touch breakpoints.
findings.evidence: Swipe is implemented (lemon.js L572-581) but invisible on mobile — users on phones may think the carousel is static, missing 2/3 of the social-proof content.

findings.title: Stat number 'S$211–1,072' has the en-dash as a separator without an aria-label
findings.recommendation: Wrap the en-dash in <span aria-hidden="true"> and add an aria-label='S$211 to 1,072' on the .stat__n parent, OR write 'to' verbatim. Screen readers say 'em dash' or skip it.
findings.evidence: Stat strip headline range in the hero immediately below the fold. The number is the buying argument; the screen reader rendering should be unambiguous.

findings.title: Every page is set to robots: noindex, nofollow — intentional pre-launch but not flagged in the audit log
findings.recommendation: Keep noindex through concept stage. When pre-launch ends, sweep all 7 pages — and stitch the OG image at assets/img/og-image.png to a real 1200×630 (current file presence not verified in audit).
findings.file_ref: /Users/derrickteo/codings/Lemon-man/index.html:8, all other pages same line, and 404.html:8
findings.evidence: Per MEMORY.md the project is concept-stage and noindex is intentional. Audit-log this so the launch checklist has a paper trail; remove noindex + verify og-image.png exists at 1200×630 as part of the launch gate.

findings.title: Body has overflow-x: hidden which masks layout bugs but reduces debuggability
findings.recommendation: Keep for production but during QA temporarily remove overflow-x:hidden and audit for horizontal-scroll-causing elements (citrus-ghost at -15vw is the prime suspect).
findings.evidence: Hero's decorative SVG explicitly overflows -15vw; overflow-x:hidden hides the resulting page-level scroll. Cosmetically fine but the workaround conceals other elements that might creep over the right edge over time.

findings.title: Gauge IntersectionObserver fires once and disconnects — second-look users on back-navigation get a static 0
findings.recommendation: Either store the computed final number as the rest state (currently the SVG dashoffset rests at 0 = full arc) or rerun on pageshow event for back/forward cache hits.
findings.evidence: go.disconnect() inside the observer + fired flag means a bfcache restore on for-workers.html (which has a hero gauge) shows the static initial state. Minor — most users will scroll away and back, triggering re-observation on the live observer that doesn't exist after disconnect.

findings.title: Scoreboard 'caught' counts on index.html use fictional agency names that look real
findings.recommendation: Add an inline 'SAMPLE' or 'Composite — pre-launch' label tied to each row (small mono tag), not just the single 'SAMPLE' in the corner — Eastside Manpower / Harbourfront Crew Co. / Reddot Event Hands / Lion City Security all read as real SG companies.
findings.evidence: The single 'SAMPLE' top-right (L253) is easy to miss; the agency names sound plausibly real for SG F&B/logistics — Boss Confessions block carries a proper 'Composites, not real individuals' disclaimer (L183) but the scoreboard does not match that standard.

findings.title: Hero quadratic claim 'corroboration multiplier — m(n) = 0.25n² + 0.75n' is great proof but is in a .note one-liner
findings.recommendation: Promote the formula to its own card / pull-out next to the quadratic example block — it's the platform's single hardest credibility lever and currently sits as a paragraph at how-it-works.html L109.
findings.evidence: The maths is verifiable (matches lemon.js L199 corr() exactly) but rendered as a sentence with bold; the quadratic example card at L156-164 is right there but doesn't show the formula. Pair them visually — proof + worked example side by side.

findings.title: Hero h1 italic 'lemons.' inherits Fraunces italic 500 — but on Windows ChromeFonts can swap if Fraunces italic axis fails to load
findings.recommendation: Add a font-display: swap fallback weight check in the @import (currently @display=swap is set, good). Ship a self-hosted Fraunces 'lemons' fallback OR accept that on slow loads the italic-display first paint is the brand carrier and pre-load explicitly: <link rel='preload' as='font' ...>.
findings.file_ref: /Users/derrickteo/codings/Lemon-man/assets/css/lemon.css:7 (Google Fonts import incl ital,opsz,wght axes); :85 (.display em)
findings.evidence: Single Google Fonts call is correct but no font-preload. The italic 'lemons.' is the single biggest brand element on the homepage; a FOUT here costs more than a FOUT on body.

findings.title: Access form success message uses lemon emoji '🍋' which screen readers announce as 'lemon'
findings.recommendation: Acceptable — the emoji aligns with brand voice ('Squeezed in.'). Just verify the polite live region announces the full sentence, not just the emoji.
findings.evidence: role='status' is set on .form-msg (index.html L282) — JAWS/NVDA will announce. Brand-aligned, low risk.

findings.title: Calculator and simulator stepper buttons have no keyboard-arrow support — users tab to a +, hit Enter or Space
findings.recommendation: Add ArrowUp/ArrowDown on the .cinc__ctl row to increment/decrement; persist focus on the active stepper. Currently each + or − needs an explicit tab-to-target.
findings.evidence: Tab order through 10 incidents × 2 steppers = 20 tabs to reach the last row's '+'. Arrow-key support would halve the keyboard cost without touching mouse UX.

findings.title: Singapore-government register on the worker bill of rights could be stronger
findings.recommendation: On for-workers.html, the 'Independent appeal' card (L185-203) could explicitly cite the panel composition (e.g. 'reviewed by a panel of at least one worker representative, one employer representative, one independent moderator') to align with the WSG/MOM tripartite register the audience trusts.
findings.evidence: Tone is correct but vague — 'a panel that is not the original employer' is the strongest claim, which is institutional-light. PDPA-first brand should commit to panel structure or label this 'process subject to launch consultation' to be honest at concept stage.

headline_findings: lm-design-003 / lm-design-001 — mid-grey ink-3 mono labels and the kicker --sour both sit at ~4.4–4.5:1 borderline AA on the cream paper across every kicker, caption, gauge-row and note — single biggest fixable defect, one-token change resolves it

lm-design-004 — the full 50-incident simulator at /simulator.html is the strongest credibility artefact and currently lives only behind a paragraph link inside How-it-works; needs a nav slot or prominent CTA

lm-design-010 / lm-design-011 — scoreboard agency names look real (need clearer 'SAMPLE' labelling) and the corroboration formula m(n)=0.25n²+0.75n is the platform's single hardest credibility proof but is buried in a footnote — promote it

audit_summary.three_intent_findability_pass_rate: 3/3 — all three buyer intents reachable in ≤4 seconds from index.html
audit_summary.verdict: Strong design with a clear and defensible voice; weakest dimension is colour-contrast (3/5) — the cream-paper system's mid-grey labels sit on the AA threshold. Lifting --ink-3 one shade darker is a one-line fix that would push the whole rubric to ~4.6/5.
